name: colormath

metadata.name: colormath
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Python Color Math Module (colormath)

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/gtaylor/python-colormath.png?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/gtaylor/python-colormath
```

```{image} https://pypip.in/d/colormath/badge.png
:target: https://crate.io/packages/colormath/
```

This module implements a large number of different color operations such as
color space conversions, Delta E, and density to spectral.

## Requirements

- numpy
- NetworkX 2.0+
- Python 2.7 or Python 3.5+

## Installation

The easiest way to install colormath is via pip/easy_install:

```
pip install colormath
```

## Documentation

For documentation, see the project webpage at:

> <http://python-colormath.readthedocs.org/>

There are also a lot of useful examples under the examples directory within
this directory.

## Support

Head over to <https://github.com/gtaylor/python-colormath>
and submit an issue if you have any problems or questions.

## Legal Mumbo Jumbo

[gregory taylor]: http://gc-taylor.com

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: examples.example_config
documentation: This file holds various configuration options used for all of the examples.

modules.name: examples.delta_e_matrix
documentation:
For a massive matrix of colors and color labels you can download
the follow two files

# http://lyst-classifiers.s3.amazonaws.com/color/lab-colors.pk
# http://lyst-classifiers.s3.amazonaws.com/color/lab-matrix.pk

lab-colors is a cPickled list of color names and lab-matrix is a
cPickled (n,3) numpy array LAB values such that row q maps to
index q in the lab color list

modules.name: examples.density
documentation: This module shows you how to perform various kinds of density calculations.

modules.name: examples.conversions
documentation:
This module shows you how to perform color space conversions. Please see the
chart on www.brucelindbloom.com/Math.html for an illustration of the conversions
you may perform.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_lab_to_xyz
documentation: This function shows a simple conversion of an Lab color to an XYZ color.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_lchab_to_lchuv
documentation:
This function shows very complex chain of conversions in action.

LCHab to LCHuv involves four different calculations, making this the
conversion requiring the most steps.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_lab_to_rgb
documentation:
Conversions to RGB are a little more complex mathematically. There are also
several kinds of RGB color spaces. When converting from a device-independent
color space to RGB, sRGB is assumed unless otherwise specified with the
target_rgb keyword arg.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_rgb_to_xyz
documentation: The reverse is similar.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_spectral_to_xyz
documentation:
Instantiate an Lab color object with the given values. Note that the
spectral range can run from 340nm to 830nm. Any omitted values assume a
value of 0.0, which is more or less ignored. For the distribution below,
we are providing an example reading from an X-Rite i1 Pro, which only
measures between 380nm and 730nm.

functions.name: examples.conversions.example_lab_to_ipt
documentation: This function shows a simple conversion of an XYZ color to an IPT color.

modules.name: examples.delta_e
documentation: This module shows some examples of Delta E calculations of varying types.

modules.name: colormath.color_exceptions
documentation: This module contains exceptions for use throughout the L11 Colorlib.

classes.name: colormath.color_exceptions.ColorMathException
documentation: Base exception for all colormath exceptions.

classes.name: colormath.color_exceptions.UndefinedConversionError
documentation: Raised when the user asks for a color space conversion that does not exist.

classes.name: colormath.color_exceptions.InvalidIlluminantError
documentation: Raised when an invalid illuminant is set on a ColorObj.

classes.name: colormath.color_exceptions.InvalidObserverError
documentation: Raised when an invalid observer is set on a ColorObj.

functions.name: colormath.chromatic_adaptation._get_adaptation_matrix
documentation:
Calculate the correct transformation matrix based on origin and target
illuminants. The observer angle must be the same between illuminants.

See colormath.color_constants.ADAPTATION_MATRICES for a list of possible
adaptations.

Detailed conversion documentation is available at:
http://brucelindbloom.com/Eqn_ChromAdapt.html
noinspection PyPep8Naming

functions.name: colormath.chromatic_adaptation.apply_chromatic_adaptation
documentation:
Applies a chromatic adaptation matrix to convert XYZ values between
illuminants. It is important to recognize that color transformation results
in color errors, determined by how far the original illuminant is from the
target illuminant. For example, D65 to A could result in very high maximum
deviance.

An informative article with estimate average Delta E values for each
illuminant conversion may be found at:

http://brucelindbloom.com/ChromAdaptEval.html
noinspection PyPep8Naming

functions.name: colormath.chromatic_adaptation.apply_chromatic_adaptation_on_color
documentation: Convenience function to apply an adaptation directly to a Color object.
noinspection PyPep8Naming

modules.name: colormath.color_diff

The functions in this module are used for comparing two LabColor objects
using various Delta E formulas.

modules.name: colormath.color_diff_matrix
documentation:
This module contains the formulas for comparing Lab values with matrices
and vectors. The benefit of using NumPy's matrix capabilities is speed. These
calls can be used to efficiently compare large volumes of Lab colors.

functions.name: colormath.color_diff_matrix.delta_e_cie1976
documentation:
Calculates the Delta E (CIE1976) between `lab_color_vector` and all
colors in `lab_color_matrix`.

modules.name: colormath.density_standards
documentation: Various density standards.

modules.name: colormath.density
documentation: Formulas for density calculation.

functions.name: colormath.density.ansi_density
documentation:
Calculates density for the given SpectralColor using the spectral weighting
function provided. For example, ANSI_STATUS_T_RED. These may be found in
{py:mod}`colormath.density_standards`.

```
param SpectralColor color

The SpectralColor object to calculate
density for.

param numpy.ndarray std_array

NumPy array of filter of choice
from .

rtype

float

returns

The density value for the given color and density standard.
```

functions.name: colormath.density.auto_density
documentation:
Given a SpectralColor, automatically choose the correct ANSI T filter.
Returns a tuple with a string representation of the filter the
calculated density.

```
param SpectralColor color

The SpectralColor object to calculate
density for.

rtype

float

returns

The density value, with the filter selected automatically.
```

modules.name: colormath.color_conversions
documentation:
Conversion between color spaces.

:::{note}
This module makes extensive use of imports within functions.
That stinks.
:::

functions.name: colormath.color_conversions.apply_RGB_matrix
documentation:
Applies an RGB working matrix to convert from XYZ to RGB.
The arguments are tersely named var1, var2, and var3 to allow for the
passing of XYZ _or_ RGB values. var1 is X for XYZ, and R for RGB. var2 and
var3 follow suite.
noinspection PyPep8Naming

functions.name: colormath.color_conversions.color_conversion_function
documentation:
Decorator to indicate a function that performs a conversion from one color
space to another.

This decorator will return the original function unmodified, however it will
be registered in the \_conversion_manager so it can be used to perform color
space transformations between color spaces that do not have direct
conversion functions (e.g., Luv to CMYK).

Note: For a conversion to/from RGB supply the BaseRGBColor class.

```
param start_type

Starting color space type

param target_type

Target color space type
```

functions.name: colormath.color_conversions.convert_color
documentation:
Converts the color to the designated color space.

```
param color

A Color instance to convert.

param target_cs

The Color class to convert to. Note that this is not
an instance, but a class.

keyword BaseRGBColor through_rgb_type

If during your conversion between
your original and target color spaces you have to pass through RGB,
this determines which kind of RGB to use. For example, XYZ->HSL.
You probably don't need to specify this unless you have a special
usage case.

type target_illuminant

None or str

keyword target_illuminant

If during conversion from RGB to a reflective
color space you want to explicitly end up with a certain illuminant,
pass this here. Otherwise the RGB space's native illuminant
will be used.

returns

An instance of the type passed in as target_cs.

raises


if conversion between the two color spaces isn't possible.
```

methods.name: colormath.color_conversions.ConversionManager.add_type_conversion
documentation:
Register a conversion function between two color spaces.
\:param start_type: Starting color space.
\:param target_type: Target color space.
\:param conversion_function: Conversion function.

methods.name: colormath.color_conversions.ConversionManager.get_conversion_path
documentation:
Return a list of conversion functions that if applied iteratively on a
color of the start_type color space result in a color in the result_type
color space.

Raises an UndefinedConversionError if no valid conversion path
can be found.

```
param start_type

Starting color space type.

param target_type

Target color space type.

return

List of conversion functions.
```

methods.name: colormath.color_conversions.ConversionManager._normalise_type
documentation:
Return the highest superclass that is valid for color space
conversions (e.g., AdobeRGB -> BaseRGBColor).

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.ColorBase.get_value_tuple
documentation:
Returns a tuple of the color's values (in order). For example,
an LabColor object will return (lab_l, lab_a, lab_b), where each
member of the tuple is the float value for said variable.

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.ColorBase.__str__
documentation: String representation of the color.

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.ColorBase.__repr__
documentation: String representation of the object.
A base class holding some common methods and values.

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.IlluminantMixin

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.IlluminantMixin.set_observer
documentation:
Validates and sets the color's observer angle.

:::{note}
This only changes the observer angle value. It does no conversion
of the color's coordinates.
:::

```
param str observer

One of '2' or '10'.
```
noinspection PyAttributeOutsideInit

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.IlluminantMixin.set_illuminant
documentation:
Validates and sets the color's illuminant.

:::{note}
This only changes the illuminant. It does no conversion
of the color's coordinates. For this, you'll want to refer to
{py:meth}`XYZColor.apply_adaptation <colormath.color_objects.XYZColor.apply_adaptation>`.
:::

:::{tip}
Call this after setting your observer.
:::

```
param str illuminant

One of the various illuminants.
```
noinspection PyAttributeOutsideInit

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.IlluminantMixin.get_illuminant_xyz
documentation:
:param str observer: Get the XYZ values for another observer angle. Must
    be either '2' or '10'.
:param str illuminant: Get the XYZ values for another illuminant.
:returns: the color's illuminant's XYZ values.
Color spaces that have a notion of an illuminant should inherit this.

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.SpectralColor

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.SpectralColor.__init__
documentation:
---
keyword str illuminant: See {doc}`illuminants` for valid values.
keyword str observer: Observer angle. Either `'2'` or `'10'` degrees.
---

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.SpectralColor.get_numpy_array
documentation: Dump this color into NumPy array.

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.SpectralColor.calc_density
documentation:
Calculates the density of the SpectralColor. By default, Status T
density is used, and the correct density distribution (Red, Green,
or Blue) is chosen by comparing the Red, Green, and Blue components of
the spectral sample (the values being red in via "filters").

A SpectralColor represents a spectral power distribution, as read by
a spectrophotometer. Our current implementation has wavelength intervals
of 10nm, starting at 340nm and ending at 830nm.

Spectral colors are the lowest level, most "raw" measurement of color.
You may convert spectral colors to any other color space, but you can't
convert any other color space back to spectral.

See `Spectral power distribution <http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spectral_power_distribution>`_
on Wikipedia for some higher level details on how these work.

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.LabColor

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.LabColor.__init__
documentation:
---
keyword str illuminant: See {doc}`illuminants` for valid values.
keyword str observer: Observer angle. Either `'2'` or `'10'` degrees.
param float lab_a: a coordinate.
param float lab_b: b coordinate.
param float lab_l: L coordinate.
---

Represents a CIE Lab color. For more information on CIE Lab,
see `Lab color space <http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lab_color_space>`_ on
Wikipedia.

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.LCHabColor

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.LCHabColor.__init__
documentation:
---
keyword str illuminant: See {doc}`illuminants` for valid values.
keyword str observer: Observer angle. Either `'2'` or `'10'` degrees.
param float lch_c: C coordinate.
param float lch_h: H coordinate.
param float lch_l: L coordinate.
---

Represents an CIE LCH color that was converted to LCH by passing through
CIE Lab. This differs from {py:class}`LCHuvColor`, which was converted to
LCH through CIE Luv.

See [Introduction to Colour Spaces](http://www.colourphil.co.uk/lab_lch_colour_space.shtml)
by Phil Cruse for an illustration of how CIE LCH differs from CIE Lab.

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.LCHuvColor

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.LCHuvColor.__init__
documentation:
---
keyword str illuminant: See {doc}`illuminants` for valid values.
keyword str observer: Observer angle. Either `'2'` or `'10'` degrees.
param float lch_c: C coordinate.
param float lch_h: H coordinate.
param float lch_l: L coordinate.
---

Represents an CIE LCH color that was converted to LCH by passing through
CIE Luv. This differs from {py:class}`LCHabColor`, which was converted to
LCH through CIE Lab.

See [Introduction to Colour Spaces](http://www.colourphil.co.uk/lab_lch_colour_space.shtml)
by Phil Cruse for an illustration of how CIE LCH differs from CIE Lab.

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.BaseRGBColor.__init__
documentation:
:param float rgb_r: R coordinate. 0...1. 1-255 if is_upscaled=True.
:param float rgb_g: G coordinate. 0...1. 1-255 if is_upscaled=True.
:param float rgb_b: B coordinate. 0...1. 1-255 if is_upscaled=True.
:keyword bool is_upscaled: If False, RGB coordinate values are
    beteween 0.0 and 1.0. If True, RGB values are between 1 and 255.

methods.name: colormath.color_objects.BaseRGBColor._clamp_rgb_coordinate
documentation:
Clamps an RGB coordinate, taking into account whether or not the
color is upscaled or not.

The coordinate value.

rtype

float

returns

The clamped value.
```

Converts the RGB value to a hex value in the form of: #RRGGBB

```
rtype

str
```

Converts an RGB hex string like #RRGGBB and assigns the values to

sRGBColor
```

Base class for all RGB color spaces.

:::{warning}
Do not use this class directly!
:::

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.sRGBColor
documentation:
Represents an sRGB color.

:::{note}
If you pass in upscaled values, we automatically scale them
down to 0.0-1.0. If you need the old upscaled values, you can
retrieve them with {py:meth}`get_upscaled_value_tuple`.
:::

```
ivar float rgb_r

R coordinate

ivar float rgb_g

G coordinate

ivar float rgb_b

B coordinate

ivar bool is_upscaled

If True, RGB values are between 1-255. If False,
0.0-1.0.
```
noinspection PyPep8Naming

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.AdobeRGBColor
documentation:
Represents an Adobe RGB color.

:::{note}
If you pass in upscaled values, we automatically scale them
down to 0.0-1.0. If you need the old upscaled values, you can
retrieve them with {py:meth}`get_upscaled_value_tuple`.
:::

```
ivar float rgb_r

R coordinate

ivar float rgb_g

G coordinate

ivar float rgb_b

B coordinate

ivar bool is_upscaled

If True, RGB values are between 1-255. If False,
0.0-1.0.
```

classes.name: colormath.color_objects.AppleRGBColor
documentation:
Represents an AppleRGB color.

:::{note}
If you pass in upscaled values, we automatically scale them
down to 0.0-1.0. If you need the old upscaled values, you can
retrieve them with {py:meth}`get_upscaled_value_tuple`.
:::

```
ivar float rgb_r

R coordinate

ivar float rgb_g

G coordinate

ivar float rgb_b

B coordinate

ivar bool is_upscaled

If True, RGB values are between 1-255. If False,
0.0-1.0.
```

classes.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.hue_angle
documentation: Predicted hue angle $\theta$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.chroma
documentation: Predicted chroma $C$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.saturation
documentation: Predicted saturation $S$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.brightness
documentation: Predicted brightness $B_r$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.colorfulness
documentation: Predicted colorfulness $M$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Nayatani95.__init__
documentation:
---
param e_o: Illuminance of the viewing field $E_o$ in lux.
param e_or: Normalising illuminance $E_or$ in lux.
param n: Noise term $n$.
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_n: X value of reference white $X_n$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_n: Y value of reference white $Y_n$.
param y_ob: |-
  Luminance factor of achromatic background as percentage $Y_o$.
  Required to be larger than 0.18.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_n: Z value of reference white $Z_n$.
---

**References**

* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.
* Nayatani, Y., Sobagaki, H., & Yano, K. H. T. (1995). Lightness dependency of chroma scales of a nonlinear
  color-appearance model and its latest formulation. *Color Research & Application*, 20(3), 156-167.

classes.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.hue_angle
documentation: Predicted hue angle $h_s$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.chroma
documentation: Predicted chroma $C_{94}$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.saturation
documentation: Predicted saturation $s$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.brightness
documentation: Predicted brightness $Q$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.colorfulness
documentation: Predicted colorfulness $M_{94}$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.lightness
documentation: Predicted colorfulness $J$.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.__init__
documentation:
---
param cct_w: |-
  Correlated color temperature of illuminant $T$.
  Will be used to approximate l_as if not supplied.
param discount_illuminant: Truth value whether discount-the-illuminant should be applied.
  Default True.
param helson_judd: |-
  Truth value indicating whether the Heslon-Judd effect should be accounted for.
  Default False.
param l_a: Adapting luminance $L_A$.
param l_as: |-
  Scotopic luminance of the illuminant $L_{AS}$.
  Will be approximated if not supplied.
param n_b: Brightness surround induction factor $N_b$.
param n_bb: |-
  Brightness background induction factor $N_{bb}$.
  Will be approximated using y_w and y_b if not supplied.
param n_c: Chromatic surround induction_factor $N_c$.
param n_cb: |-
  Chromatic background induction factor $N_{cb}$.
  Will be approximated using y_w and y_b if not supplied.
param p: Simultaneous contrast/assimilation parameter.
param s: Scotopic response to the stimulus.
param s_w: Scotopic response for th reference white.
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_b: X value of background $X_b$.
param x_p: |-
  X value of proxima field $X_p$.
  If not supplied, will be assumed to equal background.
param x_w: X value of reference white $X_W$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_b: Y value of background $Y_b$.
param y_p: |-
  Y value of proxima field $Y_p$.
  If not supplied, will be assumed to equal background.
param y_w: Y value of reference white $Y_W$.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_b: Z value of background $Z_b$.
param z_p: |-
  Z value of proxima field $Z_p$.
  If not supplied, will be assumed to equal background.
param z_w: Z value of reference white $Z_W$.
raises ValueError: if illegal parameter combination is supplied.
---

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt._adaptation
documentation:
---
param f_l: Luminance adaptation factor
param l_a: Adapting luminance
param p: Simultaneous contrast/assimilation parameter.
param xyz: Stimulus color in XYZ
param xyz_b: Background color in XYZ
param xyz_p: Proxima field color in XYZ
param xyz_w: Reference white color in XYZ
---

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt.adjust_white_for_scc
documentation:
Adjust the white point for simultaneous chromatic contrast.

```
param rgb_p

Cone signals of proxima field.

param rgb_b

Cone signals of background.

param rgb_w

Cone signals of reference white.

param p

Simultaneous contrast/assimilation parameter.

return

Adjusted cone signals for reference white.
```

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt._get_cct
documentation:
Reference
Hernandez-Andres, J., Lee, R. L., & Romero, J. (1999).
Calculating correlated color temperatures across the entire gamut of daylight and skylight chromaticities.
Applied Optics, 38(27), 5703-5709.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.Hunt._f_n
documentation: Nonlinear response function.

**References**

* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.
* Hunt, R. W. G. (2005). *The reproduction of colour*. 5th Ed., John Wiley & Sons.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.RLAB.__init__
documentation:
---
param d: Degree of adaptation $D$.
param sigma: |-
  Relative luminance parameter $\sigma$. For average surround set $\sigma=1/2.3$,
  for dim surround $\sigma=1/2.9$ and for dark surround $\sigma=1/3.5$.
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_n: X value of reference white $X_n$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_n: Y value of reference white $Y_n$.
param y_n_abs: Absolute luminance $Y_n$ of a white object in cd/m^2.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_n: Z value of reference white $Z_n$.
---

**References**

* Fairchild, M. D. (1996). Refinement of the RLAB color space. *Color Research & Application*, 21(5), 338-346.
* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.

documentation:
---
param k_1: $k_1$

param sigma: $\sigma$
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_0: X value of reference white $X_0$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_0: Y value of reference white $Y_0$.
param y_0_abs: Absolute adapting luminance $Y_0$ in cd/m^2.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_0: Z value of reference white $Z_0$.
---

**References**


* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.
* Guth, S. L. (1995, April). Further applications of the ATD model for color vision. In *IS&T/SPIE's Symposium
  on Electronic Imaging: Science & Technology* (pp. 12-26). International Society for Optics and Photonics.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.LLAB.__init__
documentation:
---
param d: Discounting-the-Illuminant factor $D$.
param f_c: Chroma induction factor $F_C$.
param f_l: Lightness induction factor $F_L$.
param f_s: Surround induction factor $F_S$.
param l: Absolute luminance of reference white $L$ in cd/m^2.
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_0: X value of reference white $X_0$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_0: Y value of reference white $Y_0$.
param y_b: Luminance factor of the background $Y_b$ in cd/m^2.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_0: Z value of reference white $Z_0$.
---

**References**

* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.
* Luo, M. R., & Morovic, J. (1996, September). Two unsolved issues in colour management-colour appearance and
  gamut mapping. In *5th International Conference on High Technology* (pp. 136-147).
* Luo, M. R., Lo, M. C., & Kuo, W. G. (1996). The LLAB (l: c) colour model.
  *Color Research & Application*, 21(6), 412-429.

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.CIECAM02.__init__
documentation:
---
param c: Exponential nonlinearity $c$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (0.69/0.59/0.525).
param d: Discount-the-Illuminant factor $D$.
param f: Maximum degree of adaptation $F$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (1.0/0.9/0.8).
param l_a: Adapting luminance $L_A$ in cd/m^2.
param n_c: Chromatic induction factor $N_c$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (1.0,0.9,0.8).
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_w: X value of reference white $X_W$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_b: Background relative luminance $Y_b$.
param y_w: Y value of reference white $Y_W$.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_w: Z value of reference white $Z_W$.
---

**References**

* CIE TC 8-01 (2004). A Color appearance model for color management systems.
  Publication 159. Vienna: CIE Central Bureau. ISBN 3-901906-29-0.
* Fairchild, M. D. (2013). *Color appearance models*, 3rd Ed. John Wiley & Sons.

documentation:
---
param c: Exponential nonlinearity $c$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (0.69/0.59/5.25).
param d: Discount-the-Illuminant factor $D$.
param f: Maximum degree of adaptation $F$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (1.0/0.9/0.8).
param l_a: Adapting luminance $L_A$ in cd/m^2.
param n_c: Chromatic induction factor $N_c$. (Average/Dim/Dark) (1.0,0.9,0.8).
param p: Simultaneous contrast/assimilation parameter.
param x: X value of test sample $X$.
param x_b: X value of background $X_b$.
param x_w: X value of reference white $X_W$.
param y: Y value of test sample $Y$.
param y_b: Y value of background $Y_b$.
param y_w: Y value of reference white $Y_W$.
param z: Z value of test sample $Z$.
param z_b: Z value of background $Z_b$.
param z_w: Z value of reference white $Z_W$.
---

methods.name: colormath.color_appearance_models.CIECAM02m1._compute_adaptation
documentation:
Modified adaptation procedure incorporating simultaneous chromatic contrast from Hunt model.

```
param xyz

Stimulus XYZ.

param xyz_w

Reference white XYZ.

param f_l

Luminance adaptation factor

param d

Degree of adaptation.

return

Tuple of adapted rgb and rgb_w arrays.
```

**References**

* Wu, R. C., & Wardman, R. H. (2007). Proposed modification to the CIECAM02 colour appearance model to include the
  simultaneous contrast effects. *Color Research & Application*, 32(2), 121-129.
